This formulation, frequently called the Tajima estimator, is often denoted by $ \pi $ in the literature.

Watterson (1975) showed that under the assumptions of neutrality and drift-mutation equilibrium, an unbiased estimator of the per-site parameter $ \theta = 4N_{e}u $ is $$ \widehat{\theta}_{S}=S/(L a_{n}) $$ (4.3a) where $$ a_{n}=\sum_{j=1}^{n-1}1/j $$ (4.3b)

Such an estimator is attractive for two reasons. First, the singletons i

The sampling variance of the singleton-based estimator is $$ \sigma^{2}(\widehat{\theta_{1}})=\frac{\theta}{n}\left(\frac{n-1}{L}+\frac{\theta[2a_{n}(n-1)-1]}{n}\right) $$ (4.6b)
